hypothesis: Incorporating humidity-aware molecular representations—specifically, embeddings that encode the ratio of primary to tertiary amine groups alongside predicted amine–water binding affinity derived from the DFT-parameterized force field—into the surrogate model will yield greater predictive accuracy and faster Bayesian optimization convergence for CO2 adsorption capacity than sequence-based polymer language model embeddings alone, because the demonstrated thermodynamic competition between H2O and CO2 for amine binding sites (ΔE ≈ 5–7 kcal/mol favoring water) constitutes a dominant structure–property relationship that current SMILES-derived token embeddings do not explicitly capture.
